questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan was a revolutionary of which organization?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan was a member of the Hindustan Republican Association (HRA).

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan was a close comrade of which fellow revolutionary?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan was the close comrade and friend of Ram Prasad Bismil.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan took part in which famous 1925 action?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan participated in the Kakori train robbery of 1925.

questions.q: At which jail was Ashfaqulla Khan executed?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan was hanged at Faizabad jail.

questions.q: In which month and year was Ashfaqulla Khan executed?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan was executed in December 1927 in the Kakori case.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan is especially remembered as a symbol of what?
questions.explanation: His close partnership with the Hindu Bismil made him an enduring symbol of Hindu-Muslim unity.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan was born in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan was born at Shahjahanpur in present-day Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Like the other Kakori revolutionaries, Ashfaqulla Khan believed in winning freedom through what?
questions.explanation: He was a revolutionary committed to armed struggle for India's independence.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan came from which town that was also home to Ram Prasad Bismil?
questions.explanation: Both Ashfaqulla Khan and Bismil hailed from Shahjahanpur.

questions.q: What relationship best describes Ashfaqulla Khan and Ram Prasad Bismil?
questions.explanation: They were devoted friends and comrades whose bond came to symbolise communal harmony.

questions.q: In which year was Ashfaqulla Khan born?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan was born on 22 October 1900 at Shahjahanpur.

questions.q: On what date was Ashfaqulla Khan hanged at Faizabad jail?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan was executed on 19 December 1927, the same day as Bismil.

questions.q: After the Kakori arrests, Ashfaqulla Khan initially escaped capture and tried to flee abroad. Where was he eventually arrested?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan evaded arrest for a time but was finally captured in Delhi.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan, like Bismil, was also fond of writing poetry under which pen name?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan wrote Urdu poetry under pen names such as 'Warsi' and 'Hasrat'.

questions.q: Which other two Kakori convicts were hanged on the same case alongside Ashfaqulla Khan and Bismil?
questions.explanation: Rajendra Lahiri and Roshan Singh were the other two revolutionaries hanged in the Kakori case.

questions.q: What was the name of Ashfaqulla Khan's elder friend and comrade who, after his arrest, allegedly tried to tempt him into betraying the cause along communal lines, which Ashfaqulla refused?
questions.explanation: The investigating officer Tasadduq Hussain reportedly appealed to Ashfaqulla on communal grounds, but Ashfaqulla firmly rejected it, affirming his loyalty to Bismil and the nation.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan came from a respectable family of which background in Shahjahanpur?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan belonged to a respectable Pathan Muslim family of Shahjahanpur.

questions.q: In the Kakori conspiracy trial, what sentence was passed on Ashfaqulla Khan?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan was sentenced to death by hanging for his role in the Kakori action.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan was drawn into revolutionary work largely through his admiration of and friendship with which Shahjahanpur figure?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan was inspired to join the revolutionary path through his deep friendship with Ram Prasad Bismil.

questions.q: The organization Ashfaqulla Khan belonged to was founded in which year?
questions.explanation: The Hindustan Republican Association was founded in 1924.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan is often described in nationalist memory as the 'comrade' of Kakori. What is one of the honorific titles popularly given to him?
questions.explanation: He is widely honoured as a Martyr of Kakori, remembered alongside Bismil.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan and his comrades carried out the Kakori robbery in which province of British India?
questions.explanation: Kakori is near Lucknow in the United Provinces, where the action took place.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan was the youngest of how many siblings in his family, the only one drawn to revolution?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan was the youngest of six children in his Shahjahanpur family.

questions.q: While in prison awaiting execution, Ashfaqulla Khan is remembered for maintaining his religious devotion in what way?
options: He regularly read the Quran and offered prayers
He undertook a hunger strike unto death
questions.explanation: In jail Ashfaqulla Khan remained a devout Muslim, reading the Quran and praying while facing the gallows calmly.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan's defence in the Kakori trial, like that of others, was hampered when some co-accused did what?
questions.explanation: Some accused turned approvers and gave evidence for the prosecution, weakening the defence in the Kakori case.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan's poetry and letters from prison expressed which recurring theme?
options: Love of the motherland and willingness to die for freedom
questions.explanation: His prison writings dwelt on patriotic sacrifice and a fervent love of the motherland.

questions.q: After the Kakori arrests, Ashfaqulla Khan reportedly considered escaping to which country to organise from abroad before being caught?
options: He sought a route abroad, possibly via the northwest, but was arrested in Delhi
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan tried to remain underground and reach safety abroad but was arrested in Delhi before he could leave India.

questions.q: Which Indian state, formed in 2000, named a district headquarters town's institutions after Ashfaqulla Khan, reflecting his regional legacy?
options: Uttar Pradesh honours him in Shahjahanpur and beyond
questions.explanation: Uttar Pradesh, his home region, commemorates Ashfaqulla Khan with memorials and institutions, including in Shahjahanpur.

questions.q: Ashfaqulla Khan reportedly told the court or wrote that he had no hatred for which group, only for colonial injustice?
questions.explanation: Ashfaqulla Khan stated his fight was against colonial oppression, not against the British people as such.

questions.q: The friendship of Ashfaqulla Khan and Ram Prasad Bismil is frequently invoked because they belonged to different faiths yet shared what?
options: The same prison cell from childhood
A common goal of independence and willingness to die together
questions.explanation: A Muslim and a Hindu, they shared one revolutionary goal and were executed the same day, embodying communal unity.
